q: The 'Standard ECG' consists of how many leads in a typical clinical setup?
e: A 12-lead ECG provides a complete 3D view of the heart's electrical activity.

q: What is 'Systemic Circulation'?
o: Flow of blood between heart and lungs
Flow of blood between heart and the rest of the body
Flow of blood within the heart muscles
e: Systemic circulation provides oxygen and nutrients to all body tissues.

q: Which organ produces the majority of 'Plasma Proteins'?
e: The liver synthesizes albumin, fibrinogen, and most globulins found in the blood.

q: The 'Hematocrit' (Hct) value measures the percentage of:
e: Hematocrit is the volume percentage of RBCs in a sample of whole blood.

q: Which antibody is found in the plasma of a person with 'Blood Group B'?
e: A person with Group B antigens will naturally produce antibodies against Group A antigens.

q: What is 'Phlebitis'?
e: Phlebitis often occurs in the legs and can lead to the formation of blood clots (Thrombophlebitis).

q: The 'Ductus Arteriosus' in a fetus connects the:
e: It allows most of the blood from the right ventricle to bypass the fetus's fluid-filled, non-functioning lungs.

q: Which type of 'Anemia' is caused by the lack of Vitamin B12 and Intrinsic Factor?
e: Pernicious anemia is a megaloblastic anemia where RBCs do not mature properly.

q: What is 'Diapedesis'?
o: Process of WBCs squeezing through capillary walls to reach infection sites
e: WBCs use diapedesis to move out of the blood into tissues to fight pathogens.

q: The 'Interventricular Septum' separates the:
e: This thick wall prevents the mixing of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood in the heart.

q: Which chemical is added to blood bags to prevent 'Clotting' during storage?
e: Citrate binds with calcium ions ($Ca^{2+}$), which are necessary for the clotting process.

q: What is 'Polycythemia'?
e: Polycythemia increases blood viscosity (thickness), which can lead to high blood pressure or clots.

q: Which component of blood is responsible for 'Humoral Immunity'?
e: B-lymphocytes produce antibodies that circulate in 'humors' (body fluids).

q: The 'Mitral Valve' is another name for the:
e: The bicuspid valve is the only heart valve with only two flaps (cusps).

q: Which of the following describes 'Laminar Flow' in blood vessels?
o: Smooth, layered flow in the center of the vessel
Flow through the heart valves only
e: In healthy blood vessels, blood moves in layers, with the fastest flow occurring in the center of the lumen.

q: The 'Spleen' is often referred to as the 'Blood Bank' because it:
o: Stores a reserve of blood and platelets for emergencies
e: The spleen acts as a reservoir for blood, which can be released into circulation during trauma or exercise.

q: Which of the following is an example of 'Primary Lymphoid Organ'?
e: Primary lymphoid organs (Bone Marrow and Thymus) are where lymphocytes are produced and matured.

q: What is the function of 'Pre-capillary Sphincters'?
o: Regulate blood flow into specific capillary beds
e: These muscular rings open or close to direct blood where it is needed most (e.g., to the gut after a meal).

q: The 'Pecquet's Reservoir' (Receptaculum Chyli) is related to which system?
e: It is a dilated sac at the lower end of the thoracic duct that collects lymph from the lower body.

q: Which condition is characterized by 'Accumulation of Fluid' in the tissues, leading to swelling?
e: Edema occurs when fluid leaks out of capillaries faster than the lymphatic system can remove it.

q: What is 'Ischemia'?
o: Reduced blood supply to a specific organ or tissue
e: Ischemia leads to a lack of oxygen and nutrients, which can cause tissue death (infarction).

q: Which large lymph duct drains three-quarters of the body's lymph into the left subclavian vein?
e: The Thoracic duct is the main collecting duct of the lymphatic system.

q: The 'Starling's Law' of the heart states that:
o: The force of contraction is proportional to the initial length of cardiac fibers
e: The more the heart is stretched (filled with blood), the more forcefully it contracts to pump it out.

q: Which type of 'Aneurysm' involves a bulging of a weakened arterial wall?
e: An aneurysm is a dangerous localized enlargement of an artery caused by a weakening of the artery wall.

q: The 'Purkinje Shift' is related to vision, but 'Purkinje Fibers' in the heart are made of modified:
e: Purkinje fibers are specialized cardiac muscle cells that conduct electrical impulses very rapidly.

q: Which of the following has the 'Thickest Tunica Media' (Muscular layer)?
e: Arteries have a thick muscular layer to withstand and maintain high blood pressure.

q: What is 'Diastolic' blood pressure?
e: Diastolic pressure (the lower number) represents the pressure in arteries when the heart is resting between beats.

q: The 'Circle of Willis' provides blood supply to the:
e: It is a ring of interconnected arteries at the base of the brain that ensures constant blood flow.

q: Which blood cell is 'Polymorphonuclear' (having multi-lobed nuclei)?
e: Neutrophils are also called 'PMNs' because their nucleus can have 3 to 5 lobes.

q: What is 'Thrombosis'?
o: Formation of a blood clot inside a blood vessel
e: A thrombus can block blood flow, leading to conditions like a stroke or heart attack.

q: The 'Coronary Sinus' carries deoxygenated blood from the:
e: It collects venous blood from the heart wall and delivers it to the right atrium.

q: Which of the following increases 'Peripheral Resistance' to blood flow?
e: Narrowing of the blood vessels (vasoconstriction) increases resistance and raises blood pressure.

q: What is the 'Double Membrane' of the heart called?
e: The pericardium consists of an outer fibrous layer and an inner serous layer with fluid in between.

q: Which of the following animals possesses a 'Two-chambered' heart (one atrium and one ventricle)?
e: Fish have a simple circuit where the heart pumps deoxygenated blood to the gills for oxygenation.

q: The 'Three-chambered' heart (two atria and one ventricle) is a characteristic of:
e: In amphibians and most reptiles, oxygenated and deoxygenated blood mix in the single ventricle.

q: Which reptile is an exception and possesses a 'Four-chambered' heart like birds and mammals?
e: Crocodiles have a completely separated four-chambered heart, preventing the mixing of blood.

q: What is the 'Foramen of Panizza'?
o: A hole in the human heart
A connection between the left and right aortas in crocodilians
A valve in the fish heart
e: This is a unique evolutionary feature in crocodiles that allows them to bypass their lungs while diving.

q: In an ECG, if the 'P-R Interval' is abnormally long, it indicates a delay in:
o: Impulse conduction through the AV Node
e: A prolonged P-R interval often suggests a 'heart block' where the signal from atria to ventricles is slowed.

q: Which condition is characterized by the presence of a 'Blood Clot' that travels through the bloodstream and blocks a distant vessel?
e: A moving clot is called an embolus; when it gets stuck, it causes an embolism (e.g., Pulmonary Embolism).

q: What is the primary function of 'Atrial Natriuretic Factor' (ANF) secreted by the heart?
e: ANF is released when the atria are stretched; it promotes salt and water excretion by the kidneys to lower blood volume.

q: The 'Vasa Vasorum' are literally translated as:
e: They are the tiny arteries that supply blood to the thick walls of large arteries and veins like the Aorta.

q: Which phase of the Cardiac Cycle is the longest in duration?
e: Joint Diastole (where all four chambers are relaxed) lasts about 0.4 seconds of the 0.8-second cycle.

q: What is 'Venous Return'?
o: Blood flowing from arteries to veins
Volume of blood flowing back to the heart through the veins per minute
e: Venous return must match cardiac output to ensure the heart has enough blood to pump out.

q: The 'Skeleton of the Heart' is made of:
e: Fibrous rings surround the valves, providing support and electrically isolating the atria from the ventricles.

q: Which of the following describes 'Cardiac Tamponade'?
o: Compression of the heart due to fluid accumulation in the pericardium
e: Fluid buildup in the pericardial sac puts pressure on the heart, preventing it from filling properly.

q: What is the role of 'Papillary Muscles'?
o: Contract to pull on Chordae Tendineae and keep valves closed
e: These muscles in the ventricles prevent the AV valves from prolapsing (inverting) during contraction.

q: Which heart sound is associated with the 'Rush of blood' into the ventricles (often heard in children or athletes)?
e: S3 is a low-frequency sound occurring early in diastole during the rapid ventricular filling phase.

q: The 'Fossa Ovalis' in the adult heart is a remnant of which fetal structure?
e: It is the depression in the interatrial septum that marks where the fetal hole used to be.

q: What is 'Blue Baby Syndrome' medically known as?
o: Cyanosis (often due to Tetralogy of Fallot)
e: It occurs when deoxygenated blood mixes with oxygenated blood, often due to a 'hole in the heart' (septal defect).

q: Which wave in the ECG is usually used to calculate the 'Heart Rate'?
e: The distance between two consecutive R-waves (R-R interval) is the standard for measuring heart rate.

q: Which type of blood vessel is 'Fenestrated' to allow for high filtration (e.g., in kidneys)?
e: Fenestrated capillaries have small pores (fenestrae) that increase their permeability.

q: What is 'Orthostatic Hypotension'?
o: A sudden drop in blood pressure when standing up
e: Gravity pulls blood to the legs, and the heart must quickly adjust to maintain flow to the brain.

q: The 'Windkessel Effect' is a term associated with the elasticity of the:
e: Elastic arteries expand and recoil, smoothing out the pressure changes from the heart's pumping.

q: Which 'Blood Group' is known as the 'Bombay Blood Group' (Hh phenotype)?
o: A very rare group lacking the H-antigen
e: Discovered in Mumbai, people with this group can only receive blood from other Bombay Blood Group donors.

q: What is 'Erythropoietin' (EPO)?
o: A hormone that stimulates RBC production
e: EPO is produced mainly by the kidneys in response to low oxygen levels in the blood.

q: The 'T-wave' represents which electrical event?
e: It represents the recovery phase of the ventricles as they prepare for the next contraction.

q: Which of the following prevents 'Blood Clotting' inside our healthy blood vessels?
e: Heparin is a natural anticoagulant produced by basophils and mast cells.

q: What is 'Lymphedema'?
o: Swelling caused by blockage in the lymphatic system
e: It often occurs after surgery or infection where lymph nodes are damaged or removed.

q: Which of the following acts as the 'Primary reservoir' for blood volume (60-70% of total blood)?
e: Veins are highly distensible and act as blood reservoirs, also called 'capacitance vessels'.

q: The 'Left Coronary Artery' is often nicknamed the 'Widow-maker' because a blockage here affects the:
e: It supplies the majority of the blood to the left ventricle, the heart's main pump.

q: In which animal does 'Single Circulation' occur?
e: In fish, blood passes through the heart only once during a complete circuit of the body.

q: What is 'P-wave' in ECG indicative of?
e: It represents the electrical impulse starting from the SA node and spreading through the atria.

q: The 'Circulatory System' was first accurately described by which scientist?
e: In 1628, William Harvey published his findings proving that blood circulates in a closed circuit pumped by the heart.

q: What is the 'Frank-Starling Law' of the heart primarily concerned with?
e: It states that the force of ventricular contraction is proportional to the initial length of the muscle fibers (preload).

q: Which of the following is the 'Universal Receiver' of plasma (not RBCs)?
e: While AB+ is the universal receiver for RBCs, O- is the universal receiver for plasma because its plasma lacks A and B antibodies.

q: The 'Bohr Effect' describes the shift in the oxygen-hemoglobin dissociation curve caused by:
e: An increase in CO2 or a decrease in pH (acidity) causes hemoglobin to release oxygen more readily to tissues.

q: Which structure in the fetal heart allows blood to flow from the 'Right Atrium' directly to the 'Left Atrium'?
e: The Foramen Ovale allows blood to bypass the non-functional fetal lungs. It closes at birth to become the Fossa Ovalis.

q: The 'Haldane Effect' refers to the property of Hemoglobin where:
o: Deoxygenation of blood increases its ability to carry CO2
e: When hemoglobin releases oxygen, it becomes a better carrier for carbon dioxide, helping in CO2 transport from tissues.

q: Which chemical is the primary 'Vasodilator' released by the endothelium to lower blood pressure?
e: Nitric oxide relaxes the smooth muscles of the blood vessels, increasing the lumen diameter.

q: What is 'Pulse Pressure'?
o: The average of Systolic and Diastolic
The difference between Systolic and Diastolic pressure
The maximum pressure in the aorta
e: For a BP of 120/80, the pulse pressure is 40 mmHg (120 minus 80).

q: Which type of 'Shock' is caused by a massive loss of blood or fluids?
e: Hypovolemic shock occurs when there isn't enough blood for the heart to pump to the organs.

q: The 'S-A Node' is located in the wall of which heart chamber?
e: It is situated in the upper wall of the right atrium, near the opening of the superior vena cava.

q: Which of the following is a 'Negative Inotropic' agent (decreases heart contractility)?
e: Acetylcholine slows down the heart and reduces the force of contraction via the vagus nerve.

q: What is the function of 'Carbonic Anhydrase' in the blood?
o: Speeding up the reaction between CO2 and water
e: Found mostly in RBCs, this enzyme is vital for transporting CO2 as bicarbonate ions.

q: The 'Ejection Fraction' (EF) is a measurement of:
o: Percentage of blood leaving the heart each time it contracts
e: A normal EF is 55-70%. Lower values often indicate heart failure.

q: Which 'Clotting Factor' is also known as the 'Christmas Factor'?
e: Deficiency of Factor IX causes Hemophilia B, also known as Christmas disease.

q: What is 'Mean Arterial Pressure' (MAP)?
e: MAP is the average pressure in a patient's arteries during one cardiac cycle; it is a better indicator of organ perfusion.

q: Which vessel is used as the 'Golden Standard' for measuring venous pressure (CVP)?
e: Central Venous Pressure (CVP) measures the pressure in the thoracic vena cava, near the right atrium.

q: In an 'Isovolumetric Contraction', which valves are closed?
e: This is a brief moment where the ventricles contract but blood hasn't moved yet because all valves are shut.

q: What is the 'Cardiac Reserve'?
o: The difference between resting and maximum cardiac output
The extra blood in the liver
The speed of the heart during sleep
e: Athletes have a very high cardiac reserve, allowing them to increase output significantly during exercise.

q: The 'Pecquet's Cistern' (Cisterna Chyli) drains lymph primarily from which part of the body?
e: It is the origin of the thoracic duct, collecting fatty lymph (chyle) from the intestines.

q: Which 'Leukocyte' is responsible for the 'Graft vs Host' reaction in transplants?
e: T-cells recognize foreign cells and initiate the immune attack that leads to transplant rejection.

q: Which type of 'Hemophilia' is known as the 'Royal Disease' and is caused by a deficiency of Factor VIII?
e: Hemophilia A is the most common form and is an X-linked recessive genetic disorder.

q: What is the 'R-R Interval' in an ECG used to measure?
e: The time between two consecutive R-waves tells the doctor the duration of one full cardiac cycle.

q: The 'Hereditary' transmission of Blood Groups follows which genetic principle?
e: ABO blood groups show co-dominance (A and B are both expressed) and are governed by three alleles: I^A, I^B, and i.

q: Which structure in the fetus becomes the 'Ligamentum Arteriosum' after birth?
e: The Ductus Arteriosus closes within days of birth, leaving behind a fibrous ligament.

q: A 'ST-segment Elevation' on an ECG is a classic clinical sign of:
e: ST-elevation (STEMI) indicates that heart muscle is currently being damaged due to total artery blockage.

q: Which 'Blood Group' contains no antibodies in its plasma?
e: Since AB+ has A, B, and Rh antigens, it does not produce antibodies against any of them.

q: What is the function of 'Plasmin' in the blood?
e: Plasmin is an enzyme that breaks down fibrin mesh, preventing clots from growing too large.

q: The 'Cardiac Index' (CI) relates the Cardiac Output to a person's:
e: CI = Cardiac Output / BSA. It helps doctors compare heart performance across different body sizes.

q: Which condition is characterized by 'Microcytic Hypochromic' RBCs?
e: In iron deficiency, RBCs are smaller (microcytic) and paler (hypochromic) because they lack hemoglobin.

q: The 'Hiss' sound or 'Murmur' heard through a stethoscope indicates:
o: Defective heart valves causing turbulent flow
e: Heart murmurs are often caused by 'leaky' valves (regurgitation) or 'narrow' valves (stenosis).

q: Which 'Immunoglobulin' (Antibody) is small enough to cross the placenta from mother to fetus?
e: IgG provides the newborn with passive immunity during the first few months of life.

q: What is the 'PR Interval' and why is it checked?
o: Time for impulse to travel from SA Node to Ventricles
e: A prolonged PR interval indicates a first-degree AV block (conduction delay).

q: The 'Christmas Disease' is the other name for which disorder?
e: Named after Stephen Christmas, the first patient identified with Factor IX deficiency.

q: Which chemical is the natural 'Antidote' to Heparin used in hospitals?
e: While Vitamin K reverses Warfarin, Protamine Sulfate is used to neutralize Heparin.

q: In 'Sickle Cell Anemia', which amino acid is replaced by Valine in the hemoglobin chain?
e: This single point mutation causes RBCs to distort into a sickle shape under low oxygen.

q: What is 'Stroke Volume' (SV) in terms of mathematical formula?
e: End-Diastolic Volume (full heart) minus End-Systolic Volume (remaining blood) equals Stroke Volume.

q: Which heart chamber receives blood from the 'Coronary Sinus'?
e: The coronary sinus collects deoxygenated blood from the heart wall and dumps it into the right atrium.

q: What is the 'Intrinsic Factor's' primary role in hematology?
e: Produced by the stomach, it is essential for the ileum to absorb B12 for RBC maturation.

q: The 'Purkinje Fibers' conduct electrical impulses at which relative speed?
e: Fast conduction ensures the ventricles contract as a single unit to maximize pumping force.

q: Which condition is characterized by 'Target Cells' in a blood smear?
e: Thalassemia is a genetic disorder affecting hemoglobin synthesis, showing characteristic target-shaped cells.

q: What is the 'Apex' of the heart?
o: The pointed bottom part (Left Ventricle tip)
e: The apex points downward and to the left, where the heartbeat is most easily felt (Apical pulse).

q: Which type of blood vessel contains 'Valves' to prevent backflow against gravity?
e: Veins have valves that work with skeletal muscles to push blood back up to the heart.

q: The 'H-antigen' is the precursor for which blood groups?
e: Every person (except Bombay group) has H-antigen, which is converted to A or B antigens.

q: What is 'Afterload'?
o: The resistance the heart must pump against (Blood Pressure)
e: Higher blood pressure increases the 'afterload', forcing the heart to work harder to open the valves.

q: Which hormone is the primary regulator of 'Erythropoiesis' (RBC production)?
e: EPO is produced by the kidneys when they detect low oxygen levels (Hypoxia).

q: The 'Q-wave' in an ECG specifically represents:
e: The Q-wave shows the electrical signal passing through the interventricular septum.

q: Which 'Leukocyte' is the largest in size in a blood smear?
e: Monocytes are the largest WBCs and eventually migrate into tissues to become Macrophages.

q: The 'Hepatic Portal Vein' is unique because it starts and ends in:
e: It begins in the intestinal capillaries and ends in the liver capillaries (sinusoids).

q: What is 'Cardiac Output' for a heart rate of 70 and stroke volume of 70 ml?
e: Cardiac Output = HR x SV. 70 x 70 = 4900 ml/min.

q: The 'Circulatory System' is a closed loop. Who proved this in 1628?
e: William Harvey's 'De Motu Cordis' correctly described the heart as a pump and blood as a circulating fluid.

q: Which vitamin is most crucial for 'Blood Clotting'?
e: Vitamin K is needed by the liver to synthesize four major clotting factors.

q: A 'Thrombus' that breaks loose and travels in the blood is called an:
e: An embolus can eventually get stuck and block blood flow to a vital organ (Embolism).

q: What is the function of 'Albumins' in the plasma?
e: Albumin keeps water inside the blood vessels; low albumin leads to swelling (Edema).

q: The 'Spleen' is considered the graveyard for:
e: Old and damaged Red Blood Cells are trapped and broken down in the spleen.

q: Which part of the heart is known as the 'Pace-setter'?
e: The Atrio-ventricular (AV) node receives and delays the signal from the SA node.

q: In which blood vessel is the 'Velocity' of blood the lowest?
e: Blood moves slowest in capillaries to allow maximum time for nutrient and gas exchange.

q: What is 'Tachycardia'?
e: Tachycardia can be caused by exercise, stress, or cardiac issues.

q: Which organ receives oxygenated blood from the 'Hepatic Artery'?
e: The liver is unique; it gets oxygen from an artery but most of its blood volume from a vein (Portal vein).

q: Which type of WBC is primarily involved in 'Allergic Reactions'?
e: Eosinophil counts rise during allergic responses and parasitic infections.

q: What is the 'Cardiac Cycle' duration at 75 beats per minute?
e: 60 seconds / 75 bpm = 0.8 seconds per cycle.

q: The 'Bundle of His' divides into right and left branches within the:
e: The branches run down the septum toward the apex of the heart.

q: Which chamber of the heart pumps blood to the 'Lungs'?
e: The right ventricle pumps deoxygenated blood through the pulmonary artery.

q: The 'Lubb' sound (S1) is caused by the closure of:
e: It marks the start of ventricular systole.

q: What is 'Hemolysis'?
e: Hemolysis releases hemoglobin into the plasma, often due to mismatched blood or toxins.

q: Which element is essential for the function of 'Hemoglobin'?
e: Iron in the ferrous state binds the oxygen molecule.

q: The 'SA Node' is known as the heart's pacemaker because it:
o: Has the fastest rate of spontaneous depolarization
e: It fires 70-80 times per minute, overriding all other potential pacemakers.

q: What is 'Atherosclerosis'?
e: Plaques made of fat and cholesterol narrow the arteries and can cause heart attacks.

q: Which blood vessel has 'Thin Walls' and valves?
e: Veins operate under low pressure and need valves to keep blood moving toward the heart.

q: The 'Universal Donor' group O- can donate to O+ because:
o: O- lacks A, B, and Rh antigens
e: Group O- blood is 'naked' and won't be rejected by any other group.

q: What is the primary function of 'WBCs'?
e: Leukocytes identify and destroy pathogens like bacteria and viruses.

q: Which of the following is the 'Primary Site' of gas exchange in the human lungs?
e: Alveoli are tiny air sacs with thin walls and a large surface area, allowing oxygen to enter the blood and CO2 to leave.

q: The 'Voice Box' in the human respiratory system is scientifically known as the:
e: The larynx contains vocal cords that vibrate to produce sound when air passes through.

q: Which structure prevents food from entering the trachea (windpipe) during swallowing?
e: The epiglottis is a flap of elastic cartilage that covers the larynx when you swallow.

q: What is the normal breathing rate of a healthy adult at rest?
e: Adults typically breathe about 12 to 16 times per minute under normal conditions.

q: Which muscle is the most important for the process of 'Inspiration' (Inhaling)?
e: When the diaphragm contracts and flattens, it increases the volume of the chest cavity, pulling air into the lungs.

q: What is 'Tidal Volume' (TV)?
o: Volume of air inspired or expired during a normal breath
Maximum air one can breathe out
Air left in lungs after a forceful breath
e: In a healthy adult, the Tidal Volume is approximately 500 ml per breath.

q: The majority of 'Carbon Dioxide' (CO2) is transported in the blood as:
e: About 70% of CO2 is carried in the form of bicarbonate ions in the plasma.

q: Which pigment in humans has a higher affinity for oxygen than adult hemoglobin?
e: Fetal hemoglobin must pull oxygen from the mother's blood, and myoglobin stores oxygen in muscles; both bind O2 more tightly.

q: What is 'Residual Volume' (RV)?
o: Volume of air remaining in lungs even after a forceful expiration
Volume of air in the trachea
e: Residual volume ensures that the lungs do not collapse and allows for continuous gas exchange.

q: Which instrument is used to measure 'Lung Volumes' and pulmonary function?
e: Spirometry is the standard test used to diagnose conditions like Asthma and COPD.

q: A condition where the alveolar walls are damaged, reducing the surface area for gas exchange, is called:
e: Emphysema is commonly caused by long-term smoking and makes it very difficult to breathe.

q: Which part of the brain contains the 'Respiratory Rhythm Center'?
e: The medulla oblongata regulates the automatic rhythm of breathing.

q: What is the 'Chloride Shift' (Hamburger's phenomenon) related to?
o: CO2 transport and ionic balance in RBCs
e: It is the movement of chloride ions into RBCs to maintain electrical neutrality when bicarbonate leaves.

q: The 'Vital Capacity' (VC) of the lungs is the sum of:
e: VC represents the maximum amount of air a person can expel from the lungs after a maximum inhalation.

q: Which gas has an affinity for hemoglobin that is 200-250 times higher than oxygen?
e: CO binds so tightly to hemoglobin that it prevents oxygen transport, leading to carbon monoxide poisoning.

q: What is 'Pneumonia'?
o: Infection causing fluid accumulation in alveoli
e: Pneumonia is an inflammatory condition of the lung affecting primarily the microscopic air sacs.

q: Which layer of the 'Pleura' is in direct contact with the lung surface?
e: The visceral pleura covers the lungs, while the parietal pleura lines the chest wall.

q: The exchange of gases between the blood and the body tissues is called:
e: Internal respiration is the unloading of O2 and loading of CO2 at the tissue level.

q: What is the function of 'Surfactant' in the alveoli?
o: Prevent alveoli from collapsing during expiration
e: Surfactant reduces surface tension, making it easier for the lungs to expand.

q: Which chemical factor is the most powerful stimulant for breathing in a healthy person?
o: High Carbon Dioxide levels (and low pH)
e: The brain is much more sensitive to CO2 buildup than to O2 lack when regulating normal breathing.

q: Which of the following describes the 'Bohr Effect'?
o: Low pH and high CO2 decrease Hemoglobin's affinity for O2
High O2 displaces CO2 from blood
e: The Bohr effect ensures that active tissues (which produce CO2 and acid) get more oxygen released from hemoglobin.

q: The 'Haldane Effect' is primarily concerned with the transport of:
e: It states that deoxygenated hemoglobin has a higher affinity for CO2, helping the blood pick up waste from tissues.

q: What is the 'Vital Capacity' (VC) of a healthy adult male?
e: Vital Capacity is the maximum amount of air one can exhale after a maximum inhalation.

q: Which enzyme in RBCs is responsible for the rapid conversion of CO2 and water into Carbonic Acid?
e: Carbonic anhydrase is one of the fastest enzymes in the body, essential for CO2 transport as bicarbonate.

q: The 'Pneumotaxic Center' which limits the duration of inspiration is located in:
e: While the rhythm center is in the medulla, the 'switch-off' point for breathing is in the pons.

q: What is the 'Dead Space' volume in the human respiratory tract?
e: Anatomic dead space is the volume of air in the conducting zone (trachea, bronchi) that does not participate in gas exchange.

q: Which shape does the 'Oxygen-Hemoglobin Dissociation Curve' typically take?
e: The sigmoid shape is due to 'cooperative binding,' where the binding of one O2 molecule makes it easier for others to bind.

q: A 'Right Shift' in the Oxygen-Dissociation curve is caused by:
e: Factors like high temperature, high CO2, and high 2,3-DPG shift the curve to the right, meaning O2 is released more easily.

q: The condition 'Mountain Sickness' (Altitude Sickness) is primarily caused by:
o: Low percentage of Oxygen in air
e: At high altitudes, the total air pressure is low, which reduces the driving force (pO2) for oxygen to enter the blood.

q: What is 'Hyperpnea'?
o: Increased depth and rate of breathing during exercise
e: Hyperpnea is a normal physiological response to meet the increased oxygen demand of the body.

q: Which cell in the Alveoli secretes 'Surfactant'?
e: Type II cells produce surfactant (lecithin) to prevent the lungs from collapsing.

q: The 'Hering-Breuer Reflex' prevents:
e: Stretch receptors in the lungs signal the brain to stop inspiration if the lungs are stretched too much.

q: Which of the following is an 'Occupational Respiratory Disorder'?
e: Silicosis and Asbestosis occur in workers exposed to dust in stone-breaking or construction industries.

q: What is 'Functional Residual Capacity' (FRC)?
e: FRC is the volume of air remaining in the lungs after a normal (tidal) expiration.

q: In which form is 20-25% of Carbon Dioxide transported in the blood?
e: CO2 binds directly to the globin part of the hemoglobin molecule, not the iron center.

q: Which part of the respiratory system is known as the 'Conducting Zone'?
e: The conducting zone filters, warms, and humidifies air but does not participate in gas exchange.

q: What is the partial pressure of Oxygen ($pO_{2}$) in the deoxygenated blood?
e: Deoxygenated blood has a $pO_{2}$ of 40 mmHg and a $pCO_{2}$ of 45 mmHg.

q: Which structure 'zips' the trachea open with C-shaped rings of cartilage?
e: Hyaline cartilage rings prevent the trachea from collapsing under the negative pressure of breathing.

q: What is 'Dyspnea'?
e: Dyspnea is often described as 'shortness of breath' and is a common symptom of heart and lung disease.

q: The 'Respiratory Membrane' (Diffusion membrane) consists of how many main layers?
e: It consists of the alveolar epithelium, the capillary endothelium, and the fused basement membrane in between.

q: Which of the following animals performs 'Cutaneous Respiration' (breathing through the skin)?
e: Earthworms use their moist skin for gas exchange, which is why they die if their skin dries out.

q: The respiratory organs in insects like 'Cockroaches' are called:
e: Insects have a network of tubes that deliver oxygen directly to the tissues, bypassing the blood.

q: Which structure in 'Birds' makes their respiratory system the most efficient among vertebrates?
e: Air sacs allow for a continuous, one-way flow of fresh air through the lungs during both inhalation and exhalation.

q: The 'Pneumatic Center' of the brain that controls the switch from inspiration to expiration is the:
e: Specifically, the pneumotaxic center in the pons limits the duration of inspiration to regulate the rate of breathing.

q: What is 'Hypoxia'?
o: Lack of adequate oxygen supply at the tissue level
e: Hypoxia can result from low atmospheric oxygen, anemia, or poor circulation.

q: Which of the following is a symptom of 'Carbon Monoxide' poisoning?
e: CO-hemoglobin (Carboxyhemoglobin) is bright red, giving the skin a characteristic flush even as the person suffocates.

q: The 'Pleural Fluid' found between the lung membranes serves what purpose?
e: The fluid acts as a lubricant, allowing the lungs to slide smoothly against the chest wall.

q: What is 'Inspiratory Capacity' (IC)?
e: IC is the total volume of air a person can breathe in starting from a normal expiration level.

q: Which part of the 'Hemoglobin' molecule does $CO_{2}$ bind to?
e: Unlike oxygen, which binds to iron, CO2 binds to the protein part, forming carbamino-hemoglobin.

q: What is 'Apnea'?
e: Sleep apnea is a common disorder where breathing repeatedly stops and starts during sleep.

q: The 'Dust Cells' found in the alveoli are actually:
e: Alveolar macrophages (dust cells) clean the lungs by engulfing dust particles and bacteria.

q: Which condition is characterized by a 'Wheezing' sound during expiration due to inflammation of bronchi?
e: Asthma is an allergic reaction that causes spasms in the smooth muscles of the bronchioles.

q: The 'Total Lung Capacity' (TLC) is roughly ________ in an average adult male.
e: TLC includes the Vital Capacity plus the Residual Volume (the air you can never breathe out).

q: Which nerve carries the signal from the brain to the 'Diaphragm' to initiate breathing?
e: Irritation of the phrenic nerve is often the cause of hiccups.

q: What happens to the 'Diaphragm' during Exhalation (breathing out)?
e: Relaxation of the diaphragm decreases the chest volume, forcing air out of the lungs.

q: Which of the following describes 'Asphyxia'?
o: Extreme decrease in oxygen and increase in CO2 in the body
e: Asphyxia leads to unconsciousness and death if not treated immediately.

q: The 'Adam's Apple' is formed by which cartilage of the larynx?
e: The thyroid cartilage is larger in males due to testosterone, creating the visible Adam's apple.

q: What is 'Partial Pressure' of a gas?
o: Pressure contributed by an individual gas in a mixture
e: Gases always diffuse from an area of high partial pressure to low partial pressure.

q: Which type of 'Hemoglobin' is found in muscle cells to store oxygen?
e: Myoglobin consists of only one polypeptide chain and has a very high affinity for oxygen.

q: Which of the following is the 'Universal' first step of respiration that occurs in both aerobic and anaerobic organisms?
e: Glycolysis takes place in the cytoplasm and does not require oxygen to break down glucose into pyruvate.

q: The total number of ATP molecules produced from one molecule of Glucose during 'Anaerobic' respiration is:
e: In the absence of oxygen, only glycolysis occurs, yielding a net gain of 2 ATP molecules.

q: Where exactly does the 'Krebs Cycle' (TCA Cycle) take place within the cell?
e: The enzymes for the Krebs cycle are located in the fluid matrix of the mitochondria.

q: Which molecule acts as the 'Final Electron Acceptor' in the Electron Transport Chain (ETC)?
e: Oxygen combines with electrons and hydrogen ions to form water, allowing the chain to continue.

q: The 'Respiratory Quotient' (RQ) of Carbohydrates is:
e: RQ is the ratio of CO2 produced to O2 consumed. For glucose, the ratio is exactly 1.0.

q: During heavy exercise, 'Lactic Acid' buildup in muscles is a result of:
e: When oxygen is low, pyruvate is converted into lactic acid to regenerate NAD+ for glycolysis.

q: Which of the following is the 'Connecting Link' between Glycolysis and the Krebs Cycle?
e: Pyruvate is decarboxylated and combined with Coenzyme A to form Acetyl CoA before entering the mitochondria.

q: The 'F0-F1 Particles' (Oxysomes) responsible for ATP synthesis are located on the:
e: These particles contain the enzyme ATP synthase, which uses a proton gradient to create ATP.

q: What is the 'RQ' value for Fats (Tripalmitin)?
e: Fats require more oxygen for oxidation relative to the CO2 they produce, resulting in an RQ less than 1.

q: Which chemical is known as the 'Energy Currency' of the cell?
e: Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP) stores and provides energy for almost all cellular activities.

q: The conversion of Pyruvic acid into Ethyl Alcohol occurs in which organism?
e: Fermentation in yeast produces ethanol and CO2, which is used in the brewing and baking industries.

q: Which of the following describes 'Oxidative Phosphorylation'?
o: Production of ATP using energy from the Electron Transport Chain
e: It is the final stage of aerobic respiration where the majority of ATP is produced.

q: How many turns of the Krebs Cycle are required for the complete oxidation of one molecule of Glucose?
e: Since one glucose produces two pyruvate molecules, the Krebs cycle must turn twice.

q: Which of the following acts as a 'Mobile Electron Carrier' in the mitochondrial membrane?
e: Cytochrome c is a small protein that moves electrons between Complex III and Complex IV.

q: What is the net gain of NADH molecules from 'Glycolysis' alone?
e: Glycolysis produces 2 ATP (net) and 2 NADH molecules per glucose.

q: The 'Cyanide' poison kills by inhibiting which part of respiration?
o: Cytochrome c oxidase (Complex IV) of ETC
e: Cyanide stops the Electron Transport Chain, preventing the cell from using oxygen to make ATP.

q: Which of the following is a 5-carbon compound in the Krebs Cycle?
e: Alpha-ketoglutarate is the only 5-carbon intermediate in the cycle.

q: What is 'Beta-Oxidation' related to?
o: Breakdown of Fatty Acids for energy
e: Fatty acids are broken down into Acetyl CoA units through beta-oxidation to enter the Krebs cycle.

q: The enzyme 'Enolase' used in Glycolysis requires which metal ion as a co-factor?
e: Magnesium is a vital co-factor for many enzymes involved in phosphate transfer.

q: Which part of the 'Atmospheric Air' remains constant in percentage during both inhalation and exhalation?
e: Nitrogen is not used by the body during respiration, so about 78-79% is inhaled and exhaled.

q: What is the primary physiological adaptation in humans staying at high altitudes for several weeks?
e: The kidneys release Erythropoietin (EPO) to increase RBC count, helping the blood carry more oxygen in low-pressure environments.

q: During the 'Electron Transport Chain', how many ATP molecules are produced from one molecule of NADH?
e: According to the classical calculation, 1 NADH yields 3 ATP, while 1 FADH2 yields 2 ATP.

q: What is 'Nitrogen Narcosis' often called by deep-sea divers?
e: At high pressure, nitrogen dissolves in cell membranes, acting like an anesthetic and causing a feeling of euphoria or drunkenness.

q: Which of the following describes 'Acapnia'?
o: Abnormally low carbon dioxide in the blood
e: Acapnia is usually caused by hyperventilation, which 'washes out' CO2 from the system.

q: The 'Respiratory Membrane' thickness is approximately:
e: The extremely thin membrane allows for rapid diffusion of gases between the alveoli and the blood.

q: Which part of the 'Hemoglobin' dissociation curve is affected by 'Carbon Monoxide' (CO)?
o: It shifts to the Left and becomes flatter
e: CO binds to heme 200x stronger than O2 and prevents the remaining O2 from being released to tissues (Left shift).

q: The total 'Net Gain' of ATP from one molecule of glucose in Aerobic Respiration is roughly:
e: Depending on the shuttle system used (Glycerol phosphate vs Malate-aspartate), the total is 36 or 38 ATP.

q: What is the 'Oxygen Debt' (Lactic Acid oxygen system)?
o: Extra oxygen needed to process lactic acid after exercise
e: After heavy exercise, the body breathes heavily to restore ATP and convert lactic acid back to glucose in the liver.

q: Which of the following is a 'Restrictive' lung disease?
e: Restrictive diseases make the lungs 'stiff,' reducing total lung capacity, unlike obstructive diseases which block airflow.

q: What is the 'P50' value in the Oxygen-Hemoglobin curve?
o: Pressure where 50% of hemoglobin is saturated with O2
e: A higher P50 means lower affinity for oxygen (the curve has shifted to the right).

q: The 'Hamburger Phenomenon' is essential for maintaining:
e: The chloride shift ensures that the exit of negative bicarbonate ions is balanced by the entry of negative chloride ions.

q: Which enzyme converts 'Glucose-6-Phosphate' back into 'Glucose' in the liver?
e: This allows the liver to release free glucose into the blood to maintain blood sugar levels during fasting.

q: What is 'Orthopnea'?
e: Orthopnea is common in patients with heart failure or pulmonary edema.

q: The 'Maximum Voluntary Ventilation' (MVV) tests the:
o: Strength of respiratory muscles and lung mechanics
e: It measures the maximum amount of air that can be inhaled and exhaled in one minute.

q: Which molecule is known as the 'Common Oxidative Pathway' for Carbs, Fats, and Proteins?
e: All three macronutrients are eventually converted into Acetyl-CoA to enter the Krebs Cycle.

q: What is the 'Respiratory Exchange Ratio' (RER) during intense exercise?
e: As exercise intensity increases, the body relies more on carbohydrates, pushing the RER toward 1.0.

q: Which condition is characterized by 'Barrel Chest'?
e: Chronic air trapping in emphysema causes the rib cage to remain partially expanded all the time.

q: The 'Venous Admixture' or 'Shunt' refers to:
o: Blood that bypasses the lungs without being oxygenated
e: A small amount of deoxygenated blood always mixes with oxygenated blood, slightly lowering the pO2 of systemic blood.

q: Which 'Complex' of the ETC is also known as 'Succinate Dehydrogenase'?
e: Complex II is the only enzyme that participates in both the Krebs Cycle and the Electron Transport Chain.

q: What is 'Kussmaul Breathing'?
o: Deep, labored gasping often seen in Diabetic Ketoacidosis
e: It is a form of hyperventilation that helps the body reduce CO2 levels to compensate for metabolic acidosis.

q: A 'Pneumothorax' refers to which condition?
o: Air in the pleural cavity causing lung collapse
e: If the chest wall is punctured, air enters the pleural space, neutralizing the negative pressure needed to keep lungs inflated.

q: What is 'Infarction'?
o: Tissue death due to inadequate blood supply
e: Myocardial infarction is the medical name for a heart attack caused by dead heart tissue.

q: The first 'Heart Transplant' was performed by:
e: Dr. Christian Barnard performed the world's first human-to-human heart transplant in 1967.

q: What is the significance of the 'QRS Complex' in an ECG?
e: The QRS complex represents the large electrical impulse triggering the ventricles to pump.

q: The 'Lacteal' in the intestinal villi helps in the absorption of:
e: Lacteals are specialized lymph vessels that transport absorbed fats into the lymphatic system.

q: What is 'CAD' in the context of the circulatory system?
e: CAD is the narrowing of the arteries that supply blood to the heart muscle.

q: In an 'AB positive' person, which antibodies are present in the plasma?
e: Since they have A, B, and Rh antigens, they cannot have antibodies against them without attacking their own blood.

q: Which condition is caused by the lack of 'Intrinsic Factor' in the stomach?
e: Without intrinsic factor, Vitamin B12 cannot be absorbed, leading to failure of RBC maturation.

q: What is the 'pH' of the Small Intestine after the addition of pancreatic juice?
e: Bicarbonate in pancreatic juice neutralizes stomach acid, raising the pH to around 8.0 for enzyme activity.

q: Which hormone inhibits 'Gastric motility' when the small intestine is full?
e: GIP signals the stomach to slow down so the intestine doesn't get overwhelmed.

q: Which protein helps in the condensation of DNA into chromosomes?
e: DNA wraps around histone proteins to form nucleosomes, which eventually pack into dense chromosomes.

q: An infection of the 'Parotid Glands' by a virus results in which disease?
e: Mumps is a viral infection that primarily affects the parotid salivary glands located near the ears.

q: The 'Granum' is a structure found inside which organelle?
e: Grana are stacks of thylakoids where the light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis occur.

q: Which hormone inhibits gastric secretion and motility?
e: Gastric Inhibitory Peptide (GIP) slows down the stomach to allow the intestine time to process fats.

q: Fat-soluble vitamins are stored primarily in which tissue?
e: Vitamins A, D, E, and K are stored in the liver and fatty (adipose) tissues.

q: The 'Unit Membrane' concept was proposed by:
e: J.D. Robertson proposed that all biological membranes share a similar trilaminar structure.

q: The movement of molecules from high concentration to low concentration is:
e: Diffusion is a passive process that helps in the exchange of gases like O2 and CO2 across the cell membrane.

q: What is the common name for the third molar teeth in humans?
e: Wisdom teeth are the last set of molars to erupt, usually between ages 17 and 25.

q: Which pigment is responsible for the red color of human blood?
e: Hemoglobin is an iron-containing protein in red blood cells that binds with oxygen.

q: Deficiency of Vitamin K leads to:
e: Vitamin K is a co-factor for enzymes that activate blood-clotting factors like prothrombin.

q: Which enzyme converts Glucose into Ethyl Alcohol during fermentation?
e: Zymase is an enzyme complex that catalyzes the fermentation of sugar into ethanol and CO2.

q: Which part of the Alimentary canal is the widest?
e: The stomach is a J-shaped, sac-like organ that is the widest part of the digestive tract.

q: Which vitamin is synthesized by bacteria in the human colon?
e: E. coli and other bacteria in the large intestine produce Vitamin K and some B-complex vitamins.

q: An abnormal frequency of bowel movement and increased liquidity of fecal discharge is:
e: Diarrhea reduces the absorption of food and can lead to severe dehydration.

q: The 'State Election Commission' conducts elections for? / 'राज्य चुनाव आयोग' किसके लिए चुनाव आयोजित करता है?
e:
1. Logic: ECI (India) bade elections karwata hai, par SEC sirf local bodies ke liye banta hai. 
2. Article: Article 243K iska zikr karta hai.

q: Who is the 'Administrative Head' of a Gram Panchayat in Bihar? / बिहार में ग्राम पंचायत का 'प्रशासनिक प्रमुख' (Sarkari Officer) कौन होता है?
e:
1. Post-Mortem: Mukhiya chuna hua hota hai (Elected), par Panchayat Secretary sarkari naukar hota hai jo records rakhta hai. 
2. Note: Ise 'Panchayat Sewak' bhi kehte hain.

q: What is the tenure of Panchayati Raj institutions in Bihar? / बिहार में पंचायती राज संस्थाओं का कार्यकाल कितना है?
e:
1. Rule: Pehli meeting se exact 5 saal tak. 
2. Dissolution: Agar pehle bhang ho jaye, toh 6 mahine ke andar election hona mandatory hai.

q: The 'Zila Parishad' Chairman in Bihar is called? / बिहार में 'जिला परिषद' के अध्यक्ष को क्या कहा जाता है?
e: 1. Hierarchy: Gram Panchayat (Mukhiya) -> Panchayat Samiti (Pramukh) -> Zila Parishad (Adhyaksha).

q: Which Article provides for the 'State Finance Commission' for Panchayats? / पंचायतों के लिए 'राज्य वित्त आयोग' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e:
1. Logic: 280 Central Finance Commission ke liye hai, 243-I State ke liye. 
2. Work: Ye tay karta hai ki state govt ka kitna paisa Panchayato ko milega.

q: The 'Gram Sabha' consists of: / 'ग्राम सभा' में कौन शामिल होते हैं?
o: All registered voters of the village (गाँव के सभी पंजीकृत मतदाता)
e:
1. Core Concept: Direct Democracy. 
2. Fact: Har wo insaan jiska naam voter list mein hai, wo Gram Sabha ka member hota hai. 
3. Power: Ye Panchayat ke kamo ka audit karti hai.

q: How many functional items are listed in the '11th Schedule' of the Constitution? / संविधान की '11वीं अनुसूची' में कितने कार्यों की सूची दी गई है?
e:
1. Logic: Panchayato ko 29 subjects par kaam karne ki power di gayi hai (jaise Agriculture, Health, Road). 
2. Municipalities: Unke liye 12th Schedule mein 18 items hain.

q: Who appoints the 'State Election Commissioner' of Bihar? / बिहार के राज्य चुनाव आयुक्त की नियुक्ति कौन करता है?
e:
1. Logic: Local bodies ke saare head Governor hi appoint karta hai. 
2. Note: Use hatane ka process High Court ke judge jaisa tough hota hai.

q: In Bihar, what is the 'Population Criteria' for forming a Gram Panchayat? / बिहार में ग्राम पंचायत बनाने के लिए 'जनसंख्या मानदंड' क्या है?
e:
1. Bihar Rule: Bihar Panchayat Raj Act ke hisab se lagbhag 7,000 ki abadi par ek Gram Panchayat banti hai. 
2. Ward: Lagbhag 500 ki abadi par ek 'Ward' banta hai.

q: Who is the 'Chief' of the Gram Katchery in Bihar? / बिहार में ग्राम कचहरी का 'प्रधान' कौन होता है?
e:
1. Role: Sarpanch kanooni mamlo ko dekhta hai. 
2. Support: Sarpanch ki help ke liye ek 'Nyay Mitra' (Law expert) hota hai.

q: Which part of the Constitution deals with 'The Municipalities'? / संविधान का कौन सा भाग 'नगर पालिकाओं' से संबंधित है?
e:
1. Structure: Part IX (Panchayat), Part IX-A (Municipalities). 
2. Addition: Ise 74th Amendment (1992) se joda gaya.

q: The 'District Planning Committee' (DPC) is mentioned in which Article? / 'जिला योजना समिति' का उल्लेख किस अनुच्छेद में है?
e: 1. Post-Mortem: DPC ka kaam Zila Parishad aur Municipalities ke plans ko milakar ek 'Draft Development Plan' banana hota hai.

q: Who elects the 'Up-Mukhiya' (Deputy Mukhiya) in a Gram Panchayat in Bihar? / बिहार में ग्राम पंचायत में 'उप-मुखिया' का चुनाव कौन करता है?
o: Elected Ward Members among themselves (निर्वाचित वार्ड सदस्यों द्वारा अपने बीच से)
e:
1. Post-Mortem Logic: Mukhiya ka election 'Direct' hota hai (janata gunti hai). 
2. Process: Par Up-Mukhiya ka election 'Indirect' hota hai, jahan Ward Members apne beech se ek ko chunte hain. 
3. Tie: Agar vote barabar ho jayein, toh faisla 'Lottery' se hota hai.

q: Which Article provides for 'Reservation of Seats' for SC, ST and Women in Panchayats? / पंचायतों में SC, ST और महिलाओं के लिए 'सीटों के आरक्षण' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e:
1. Core Concept: Social Justice. 
2. Logic: 243-D (D for Dalits/Disadvantaged) reservation se deal karta hai. 
3. Fact: Bihar mein isi Article ke tehat BC (Backwards) ko bhi reservation diya gaya hai.

q: What is the 'Maximum Term' for which a Panchayat can be dissolved? / किसी पंचायत को अधिकतम 'कितनी अवधि' के लिए भंग रखा जा सकता है?
e:
1. Rule: Article 243-E. Agar Panchayat bhang hoti hai, toh 6 mahine ke andar election karwana mandatory hai. 
2. Note: Agar term khatam hone mein sirf 6 mahine bache hon, toh naya election zaroori nahi hota.

q: Who is the 'Political Head' of a Municipal Corporation (Nagar Nigam) in Bihar? / बिहार में नगर निगम का 'राजनीतिक प्रमुख' कौन होता है?
e:
1. Structure: Nagar Nigam ka elected head Mayor hota hai. 
2. Update: Bihar mein ab Mayor aur Deputy Mayor ka election janata 'Direct' (seedhe) karti hai. 
3. Fact: Commissioner sarkari (IAS) officer hota hai.

q: Under Article 243-S, 'Wards Committees' are mandatory for municipalities having population of? / अनुच्छेद 243-S के तहत, कितनी जनसंख्या वाली नगर पालिकाओं के लिए 'वार्ड समितियां' अनिवार्य हैं?
o: 3 Lakh or more (3 लाख या अधिक)
e:
1. Post-Mortem: Badi cities mein behtar management ke liye Ward Committees banai jati hain. 
2. Logic: Ye janata ki samasyaon ko Ward level par hi suljhane ke liye hoti hain.

q: Who provides the 'Nyay Mitra' (Law Assistant) to help the Sarpanch in Bihar? / बिहार में सरपंच की सहायता के लिए 'न्याय मित्र' कौन प्रदान करता है?
e:
1. Bihar Special: Nyay Mitra ek kanooni expert (vakeel) hota hai jise sarkar appoint karti hai taaki Sarpanch kanoon ke hisab se faisle le sake. 
2. Fact: Iska kaam sirf 'Salah' dena hota hai.

q: The '12th Schedule' of the Constitution lists how many functional items for Municipalities? / संविधान की '12वीं अनुसूची' में नगर पालिकाओं के लिए कितने कार्यों की सूची है?
e:
1. Comparison: 11th Schedule (Panchayat) - 29 items. 12th Schedule (Municipalities) - 18 items. 
2. Subjects: Isme Fire services, Urban forestry, Slum improvement jaise kaam shamil hain.

q: Which Article provides for the 'Constitution of Finance Commission' to review financial position of Panchayats? / पंचायतों की वित्तीय स्थिति की समीक्षा के लिए 'वित्त आयोग' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
o: Both A and B (A और B दोनों)
e:
1. Post-Mortem: 243-I Panchayato ke liye hai aur 243-Y Municipalities ke liye. 
2. Period: Governor ise har 5 saal mein banata hai.

q: Who has the power to 'Remove' the Mukhiya in Bihar if he is found guilty of misconduct? / यदि मुखिया कदाचार का दोषी पाया जाता है, तो बिहार में उसे 'हटाने' की शक्ति किसके पास है?
e:
1. Power: Bihar Panchayat Raj Act ke tehat, sarkar (Department of Panchayati Raj) jaanch ke baad Mukhiya ko pad se hata sakti hai. 
2. Appeal: Mukhiya iske khilaaf court ja sakta hai.

q: The 'Panchayat Samiti' in Bihar is the ________ level of the Panchayati Raj system. / बिहार में 'पंचायत समिति' पंचायती राज व्यवस्था का ________ स्तर है।
e:
1. Structure: Gram Panchayat (Ground) -> Panchayat Samiti (Middle) -> Zila Parishad (Top). 
2. Logic: Ye gaon aur zile ke beech ka bridge hai.

q: Under Article 243-Q, a 'Nagar Panchayat' is formed for? / अनुच्छेद 243-Q के तहत, 'नगर पंचायत' किसके लिए बनाई जाती है?
o: Transition areas from rural to urban (ग्रामीण से शहरी संक्रमणकालीन क्षेत्र)
e: 1. Post-Mortem: 3 types of Municipalities: 1. Nagar Panchayat (Transition gaon se shehar). 2. Nagar Parishad (Chote shehar). 3. Nagar Nigam (Bade shehar).

q: What is the 'Audit' authority of the Gram Sabha in Bihar? / बिहार में ग्राम सभा की 'ऑडिट' (अंकेक्षण) शक्ति क्या है?
e:
1. Core Concept: Social Audit. 
2. Logic: Gram Sabha ye check karti hai ki jo paisa gaon ke liye aaya, wo sach mein kharch hua ya nahi. 
3. Importance: Isse bhrashtachar par rok lagti hai.

q: Who provides the 'Voter List' for Panchayat elections in Bihar? / बिहार में पंचायत चुनावों के लिए 'मतदाता सूची' कौन उपलब्ध कराता है?
e:
1. Logic: SEC ki apni voter list hoti hai jo ECI ki list se alag ho sakti hai. 
2. Responsibility: Article 243-K ke tehat ye SEC ka kaam hai.

q: Can a person be a member of both 'Zila Parishad' and 'Legislative Assembly' (MLA) at once? / क्या कोई व्यक्ति एक साथ 'जिला परिषद' और 'विधानसभा' का सदस्य हो सकता है?
e:
1. Rule: Dual membership par rok hai. Agar koi MLA ban jata hai, toh use apni local body ki seat chhodni padegi. 
2. Logic: Ek insaan do jagah ka budget aur power control nahi kar sakta.

q: The '73rd Amendment' does NOT apply to which of the following states? / 73वां संशोधन निम्नलिखित में से किस राज्य पर लागू नहीं होता है?
e:
1. Post-Mortem: Article 243-M ke tehat in states ko chhoot mili hai kyunki wahan unki apni tribal systems hain. 
2. Special Fact: West Bengal ka 'Darjeeling District' bhi isse bahar hai.

q: Who is the 'Executive Officer' of a Municipal Corporation in Bihar? / बिहार में नगर निगम का 'कार्यकारी अधिकारी' कौन होता है?
e:
1. Logic: Mayor 'Head' hai, par asli administrative kaam Municipal Commissioner (IAS rank) karta hai. 
2. Analogy: Jaise CM aur Chief Secretary.

q: Under Article 243-W, who has the power to 'Empower' Municipalities with duties? / अनुच्छेद 243-W के तहत, नगर पालिकाओं को शक्तियों से संपन्न करने की शक्ति किसके पास है?
e: 1. Federal Logic: Local bodies 'State List' ka subject hain, isliye Assembly hi tay karti hai ki unhe kitni power deni hai.

q: What happens if a Panchayat is dissolved with only 4 months of its term remaining? / यदि किसी पंचायत को उसके कार्यकाल के केवल 4 महीने शेष रहते भंग कर दिया जाए तो क्या होगा?
o: No immediate election required (तत्काल चुनाव की आवश्यकता नहीं)
e:
1. Rule: Agar 6 mahine se kam time bacha hai, toh alag se by-election karwane ki zaroorat nahi hai. 
2. Note: Seedha naya general election hi hoga.

q: Which Article provides for the 'District Planning Committee' (DPC)? / 'जिला योजना समिति' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e:
1. Post-Mortem: DPC shehari aur gramin dono plans ko milakar poore zile ka plan banati hai. 
2. Structure: Isme 4/5 members elected hote hain.

q: The 'Gram Katchery' can hear both Civil and Criminal cases. True or False? / ग्राम कचहरी दीवानी और आपराधिक दोनों मामलों की सुनवाई कर सकती है।
e:
1. Power: Bihar mein Sarpanch dono tarah ke chhote cases sun sakta hai. 
2. Limit: Par wo kisi ko jail nahi bhej sakta, sirf fine (jurmana) laga sakta hai.

q: Who decides the 'Boundaries' of a Ward in a Bihar Municipal Corporation? / बिहार नगर निगम में वार्ड की 'सीमाओं' का निर्धारण कौन करता है?
e: 1. Fact: Ward ka delimitation (parisiman) Bihar sarkar ka Urban Development department karta hai.

q: Under Article 243-G, what is the 'Panchayat's Responsibility'? / अनुच्छेद 243-G के तहत पंचायत की 'जिम्मेदारी' क्या है?
o: Economic development and social justice (आर्थिक विकास और सामाजिक न्याय)
e: 1. Mission: Panchayato ko sirf galiyan banane ke liye nahi, balki gaon ke vikas ke liye banaya gaya hai.

q: Who is the 'Chief' of the Panchayat Samiti in Bihar? / बिहार में पंचायत समिति का 'प्रमुख' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Status: Pramukh ko Block level ke saare Mukhiya aur Samiti members chunte hain.

q: Can the 'State Election Commissioner' be removed by the Governor easily? / क्या राज्यपाल राज्य चुनाव आयुक्त को आसानी से हटा सकता है?
o: No, only on grounds and manner of a High Court Judge (नहीं, केवल HC जज के समान तरीके से)
e:
1. Security: SEC ko independence di gayi hai taaki wo bina dare election karwa sake. 
2. Method: Removal ke liye President aur Parliament ka involvement zaroori hota hai.

q: The 'Nagar Nigam' (Municipal Corporation) is established for population above? / नगर निगम की स्थापना कितनी जनसंख्या से ऊपर के लिए की जाती है? (Generic)
o: As decided by the State (राज्य द्वारा निर्धारित)
e:
1. Logic: Samvidhan ginti nahi batata, ye State Govt ke upar chhod diya gaya hai. 
2. Bihar Context: Bihar mein Patna, Gaya, Bhagalpur jaise bade sheharo mein Nagar Nigam hai.

q: Who is the 'Secretary' of the Zila Parishad in Bihar? / बिहार में जिला परिषद का 'सचिव' कौन होता है?
o: DDC (Deputy Development Commissioner - उप विकास आयुक्त)
e: 1. Post-Mortem: Zila Parishad ka executive kaam DDC dekhta hai jo IAS rank ka hota hai.

q: Under Article 243-B, 'Panchayats' are mandatory for states having population above? / अनुच्छेद 243-B के तहत, कितनी जनसंख्या से ऊपर के राज्यों के लिए पंचायतें अनिवार्य हैं?
e: 1. Rule: Jis state ki abadi 20 lakh se kam hai, wahan 3-tier system hona zaroori nahi hai (Middle level skip ho sakta hai).

q: Which body is responsible for 'Audit' of Municipal accounts? / नगर पालिका खातों के 'अंकेक्षण' के लिए कौन जिम्मेदार है?
o: State Government as per Law (कानून के अनुसार राज्य सरकार)
e: 1. Fact: State Legislature tay karta hai ki audit kaise aur kaun karega.

q: Who is the 'First Citizen' of a City in Bihar? / बिहार में किसी शहर का 'प्रथम नागरिक' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Protocol: Shehar ka pehla sammanit nagrik Mayor hota hai.

q: The 'Gram Sabha' must meet at least how many times in a year in Bihar? / बिहार में ग्राम सभा की बैठक साल में कम से कम कितनी बार होनी चाहिए?
e: 1. Bihar Rule: Bihar Panchayat Raj Act ke tehat saal mein kam se kam 4 meetings (har 3 mahine mein ek) honi zaroori hain.

q: Under the Bihar Panchayat Raj Act 2006, what is the percentage of seats reserved for EBCs (BC-I)? / बिहार पंचायत राज अधिनियम 2006 के तहत, EBC (BC-I) के लिए कितने प्रतिशत सीटें आरक्षित हैं?
e:
1. Bihar Special: Bihar ne pichde vargo (EBC) ko 20% arakshan diya hai. 
2. Limit: Ye arakshan Mukhiya, Pramukh aur Adhyaksha sabhi pado par lagu hota hai. 
3. Fact: Isse samaj ke dabbe hue vargo ko netritva (leadership) milta hai.

q: Who elects the 'Up-Sarpanch' in the Gram Katchery of Bihar? / बिहार की ग्राम कचहरी में 'उप-सरपंच' का चुनाव कौन करता है?
o: Elected Panches among themselves (निर्वाचित पंचों द्वारा अपने बीच से)
e:
1. Process: Sarpanch ka chunav direct hota hai, par Up-Sarpanch ko elected Panches milkar chunte hain. 
2. Role: Sarpanch ki অনুপস্থিতi (absence) mein ye Katchery ki adhyakshta karta hai.

q: The 'Panchayat Samiti' (Block Level) is accountable for its functions to? / पंचायत समिति अपने कार्यों के लिए किसके प्रति जवाबदेह होती है?
e:
1. Hierarchy: 3-tier system mein niche wali body upar wali ko report karti hai. 
2. Path: Gram Panchayat -> Panchayat Samiti -> Zila Parishad.

q: Article 243-F deals with the 'Disqualifications' for membership. Who is the final authority? / अनुच्छेद 243-F सदस्यता के लिए 'अयोग्यता' से संबंधित है। अंतिम अधिकारी कौन है?
o: Authority prescribed by State Law (राज्य कानून द्वारा निर्धारित अधिकारी)
e:
1. Core Concept: Authority. 
2. Bihar Context: Bihar mein election ke baad disqualification par DM ya prescribed authority faisla leti hai. 
3. Age: 21 saal se kam age hona sabse bada ground hai.

q: What is the 'Population' limit for a 'Nagar Parishad' (Municipal Council) in Bihar? / बिहार में 'नगर परिषद' के लिए जनसंख्या सीमा क्या है?
e:
1. Structure: Nagar Panchayat (12k-40k), Nagar Parishad (40k-2 Lakh), Nagar Nigam (Above 2 Lakh). 
2. Logic: Ye shehar ki abadi par depend karta hai.

q: Who is the 'Chief Executive Officer' of the Zila Parishad in Bihar? / बिहार में जिला परिषद का 'मुख्य कार्यकारी अधिकारी' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Post-Mortem: Elected head Adhyaksha hota hai, par administrative head DDC (Deputy Development Commissioner) hota hai jo IAS rank ka hota hai.

q: Which Article provides for the 'Powers, Authority and Responsibilities' of Municipalities? / नगर पालिकाओं की 'शक्तियों और जिम्मेदारियों' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e:
1. Logic: 243-G Panchayato ke liye hai aur 243-W Municipalities ke liye. 
2. Scope: Isme 12th Schedule ke 18 subjects shamil hain.

q: The 'Nyay Mitra' in Bihar must be a graduate in? / बिहार में 'न्याय मित्र' को किसमें स्नातक होना चाहिए?
e:
1. Requirement: Chunki Nyay Mitra ko Sarpanch ko kanooni salah deni hoti hai, isliye uska 'Law Graduate' hona zaroori hai. 
2. Fact: Ye ek contractual sarkari pad hai.

q: Under Article 243-E, if a Panchayat is dissolved, election MUST be held within? / अनुच्छेद 243-E के तहत, यदि पंचायत भंग होती है, तो चुनाव कितने समय में होने चाहिए?
e:
1. Rule: Samvidhan kehta hai ki seat 6 mahine se zyada khali nahi reh sakti. 
2. Note: Nayi Panchayat bache hue samay (remainder term) ke liye hi kaam karegi.

q: Who has the power to 'Divide' a Gram Panchayat into Wards in Bihar? / बिहार में ग्राम पंचायत को 'वार्डों' में विभाजित करने की शक्ति किसके पास है?
e:
1. Power: DM (Collector) hi wards ki boundary aur ginti tay karta hai. 
2. Logic: Lagbhag 500 ki abadi par ek ward banaya jata hai.

q: The '74th Constitutional Amendment' came into force on? / 74वां संविधान संशोधन कब लागू हुआ?
e:
1. Date: 73rd Amendment 24 April ko aur 74th Amendment 1 June ko lagu hua tha. 
2. Goal: Urban local governance ko mazboot karna.

q: Who is the 'Guardian' of the Gram Panchayat's properties and funds? / ग्राम पंचायत की संपत्ति और धन का 'अभिभावक' कौन होता है?
e:
1. Role: Mukhiya hi executive head hai aur sari execution ki zimmedari uski hoti hai. 
2. Fact: Paisa nikalne ke liye Mukhiya aur Panchayat Secretary dono ke sign chahiye hote hain.

q: Under Article 243-T, 'Women Reservation' in Municipalities is? / अनुच्छेद 243-T के तहत नगर पालिकाओं में 'महिला आरक्षण' कितना है?
o: Not less than 1/3 (1/3 से कम नहीं)
e:
1. Central Law: 74th Amendment kam se kam 33% kehta hai. 
2. Bihar Context: Bihar ne ise badha kar 50% kar diya hai.

q: Who acts as the 'Executive Officer' (E.O.) of a Nagar Parishad in Bihar? / बिहार में नगर परिषद के 'कार्यकारी अधिकारी' (E.O.) के रूप में कौन कार्य करता है?
o: Executive Officer appointed by Govt (सरकार द्वारा नियुक्त कार्यपालक पदाधिकारी)
e: 1. Post-Mortem: Nagar Nigam mein 'Commissioner' hota hai, Nagar Parishad/Panchayat mein 'Executive Officer' hota hai.

q: The 'PESA Act' (1996) extends Panchayati Raj to? / PESA अधिनियम (1996) पंचायती राज को कहाँ तक विस्तारित करता है?
e:
1. Full Form: Provisions of the Panchayats (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act. 
2. Logic: Ye tribal logo ki paramparao ko bachate hue unhe self-rule deta hai.

q: Who is the 'Chief' of the Zila Parishad elected by members in Bihar? / बिहार में सदस्यों द्वारा निर्वाचित जिला परिषद का 'प्रमुख' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Structure: Zila Parishad ke elected members apne beech se ek Adhyaksha aur ek Up-Adhyaksha chunte hain.

q: Article 243-C deals with the 'Composition' of Panchayats. Who decides the number of seats? / अनुच्छेद 243-C पंचायतों की 'बनावट' से संबंधित है। सीटों की संख्या कौन तय करता है?
o: The State Legislature by Law (राज्य विधानमंडल कानून द्वारा)
e: 1. Power: Har state ki Assembly khud tay karti hai ki unke yahan kitne ward ya kitni seats hongi.

q: The 'Gram Katchery' in Bihar has a 'Secretary' (Sarkari). Who is he? / बिहार में ग्राम कचहरी का एक 'सचिव' होता है। वह कौन है?
e: 1. Distinction: Gram Panchayat ke liye 'Panchayat Secretary' hota hai, aur Gram Katchery ke liye 'Nyay Secretary' (Justice Secretary) hota hai.

q: Which Article provides for the 'Audit of Accounts' of Municipalities? / नगर पालिकाओं के 'खातों के अंकेक्षण' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e:
1. Logic: 243-J (Panchayat audit), 243-Z (Municipality audit). 
2. Importance: Isse bhrashtachar par lagaam lagti hai.

q: In Bihar, 'Ward Parshad' (Ward Councillor) is elected for? / बिहार में 'वार्ड पार्षद' का चुनाव किसके लिए होता hai?
e: 1. Terminology: Gaon mein 'Ward Member' hota hai, shehar mein 'Ward Parshad' (Councillor) hota hai.

q: Under Article 243-R, members of Municipalities are elected by? / अनुच्छेद 243-R के तहत, नगर पालिकाओं के सदस्य किसके द्वारा चुने जाते हैं?
o: Direct Election by the people (जनता द्वारा प्रत्यक्ष चुनाव)
e: 1. Rule: Municipal areas ko wards mein baanta jata hai aur har ward ki janata apna Councillor pratyaksh (direct) chunti hai.

q: Who is the 'Presiding Officer' of the meeting of Panchayat Samiti in Bihar? / बिहार में पंचायत समिति की बैठक की अध्यक्षता कौन करता है?
e:
1. Hierarchy: Block level par elected head 'Pramukh' hi meeting chair karta hai. 
2. Absence: Pramukh nahi ho toh Up-Pramukh meeting leta hai.

q: The 'District Planning Committee' (DPC) must have what fraction of elected members? / जिला योजना समिति (DPC) में कितने निर्वाचित सदस्य होने चाहिए?
e:
1. Rule: Article 243-ZD. DPC ke 4/5 members Zila Parishad aur Municipalities ke elected sadasyo dwara chune jate hain. 
2. Logic: Isse local planning mein elected leaders ka dawab rehta hai.

q: What is the 'Minimum Age' to vote in Panchayat elections in Bihar? / बिहार में पंचायत चुनाव में वोट देने के लिए 'न्यूनतम आयु' क्या है?
e:
1. Rule: Vote dene ka haq 18 saal par mil jata hai (Universal Adult Suffrage). 
2. Election Ladna: Chunav ladne ke liye 21 saal hona zaroori hai.

q: Who was the Chairman of the committee that suggested 'Nyaya Panchayats'? / 'न्याय पंचायतों' का सुझाव देने वाली समिति के अध्यक्ष कौन थे?
e:
1. History: 1977 mein Ashok Mehta committee ne judicial powers wali Nyaya Panchayato ki baat ki thi. 
2. Bihar Context: Bihar ne ise 'Gram Katchery' ke roop mein apnaya hai.

q: Under Article 243-H, who can authorize a Panchayat to levy and collect 'Taxes'? / अनुच्छेद 243-H के तहत, पंचायत को कर लगाने और एकत्र करने के लिए कौन अधिकृत कर सकता है?
e: 1. Power: State Assembly kanoon banakar Panchayato ko mela tax, bazar tax ya safai tax lene ki power deti hai.

q: In Bihar, 'Nagar Nigam' (Municipal Corporation) is formed for a population above? / बिहार में 'नगर निगम' कितनी जनसंख्या से ऊपर के लिए बनाया जाता है?
e: 1. Bihar Rule: Bihar Municipal Act ke tehat, lagbhag 2 lakh se zyada abadi wale sheharo ko Nagar Nigam ka darja milta hai.

q: Who is the 'Convenor' of the Gram Sabha meetings in Bihar? / बिहार में ग्राम सभा की बैठकों का 'संयोजक' कौन होता है?
e:
1. Responsibility: Meeting bulana aur agenda set karna Mukhiya ka kaam hai. 
2. Record: Minutes likhne ka kaam Secretary karta hai.

q: Which Article provides for the 'Constitution of Municipalities'? / नगर पालिकाओं के गठन का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e: 1. Logic: 243-Q hi teen tarah ki Municipalities (Nagar Panchayat, Parishad, Nigam) banata hai.

q: The 'State Election Commission' is an independent body. True or False? / राज्य निर्वाचन आयोग एक स्वतंत्र निकाय है। सही या गलत?
e: 1. Independence: Ise wahi status mila hai jo ECI ko mila hai, taaki local elections nishpaksh (fair) hon.

q: In Bihar, 'Sarpanch' can sentence a person to jail. True or False? / बिहार में सरपंच किसी को जेल की सजा सुना सकता है।
o: False (गलत - केवल जुर्माना लगा सकता है)
e: 1. Power Limit: Sarpanch sirf financial fine (jurmana) laga sakta hai. Uske paas kisi ko jail (imprisonment) bhejne ki power nahi hai.

q: Who is the 'First Citizen' of the city in a Municipal Corporation? / नगर निगम में शहर का 'प्रथम नागरिक' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Protocol: Shehar ke elected head (Mayor) ko hi pehla nagrik mana jata hai.

q: Which Schedule contains the '18 items' for Municipalities? / किस अनुसूची में नगर पालिकाओं के लिए '18 विषय' शामिल हैं?
e: 1. Fact: 11th (Panchayat-29), 12th (Municipality-18).

q: Under Article 243-U, what is the duration of a Municipality? / अनुच्छेद 243-U के तहत नगर पालिका की अवधि क्या है?
e:
1. Rule: Pehli meeting se 5 saal. 
2. Dissolution: Bhang hone par 6 mahine mein election zaroori.

q: Who is the 'Chief Executive' of a Nagar Parishad in Bihar? / बिहार में नगर परिषद का 'मुख्य कार्यकारी' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Logic: Elected head Chairman hai, par sarkari head Executive Officer (E.O.) hota hai.

q: Which Article provides for 'Metropolitan Planning Committees'? / 'महानगर योजना समितियों' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e: 1. Fact: Bade metropolitan cities ke liye ye special committees banti hain.

q: Who decides the 'Salary and Allowances' of Panchayat members in Bihar? / बिहार में पंचायत सदस्यों का 'वेतन और भत्ता' कौन तय करता है?
e: 1. Power: Bihar sarkar ne Mukhiya, Sarpanch aur baaki sadasyo ke liye ek fixed 'Maandey' (honourarium) tay kiya hai.

q: Is 'Gram Sabha' a permanent body? / क्या ग्राम सभा एक स्थायी निकाय है?
e:
1. Nature: Gram Sabha kabhi bhang nahi hoti kyunki gaon ke log hamesha wahan rehte hain. 
2. Logic: Panchayat bhang ho sakti hai, Sabha nahi.

q: Who is the 'Head' of the District Planning Committee? / जिला योजना समिति का 'प्रमुख' कौन होता hai?
o: Chairman of Zila Parishad (जिला परिषद का अध्यक्ष)
e: 1. Post-Mortem: Zyadatar states mein Zila Parishad ka Adhyaksha hi iska head hota hai taaki democratic planning ho sake.

q: Under Article 243-D, reservation of seats for Women is? / अनुच्छेद 243-D के तहत महिलाओं के लिए सीटों का आरक्षण है:
o: Not less than 1/3 (1/3 से कम नहीं)
e:
1. Central Law: Kam se kam 33%. 
2. Bihar: 50% reservation dene wala pehla state.

q: Who was the 'Father of Local Self Government' in British India? / ब्रिटिश भारत में 'स्थानीय स्वशासन का जनक' कौन था?
e: 1. History: 1882 mein Lord Ripon ne local bodies ke liye pehla bada kadam uthaya tha.

q: The 'Municipal Commissioner' is usually a member of? / नगर आयुक्त आमतौर पर किसका सदस्य होता है?
e: 1. Status: Ye ek senior IAS officer hota hai jise sarkar appoint karti hai.

q: Which Article provides for the 'Duration of Panchayats'? / पंचायतों की 'अवधि' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e: 1. Logic: E for Expiration/End (5 saal ka term).

q: Can the 'Sarpanch' be removed by a 'No-Confidence Motion' in Bihar? / क्या बिहार में सरपंच को 'अविश्वास प्रस्ताव' द्वारा हटाया जा सकता है?
e: 1. Process: Agar 2/3 Panches uske khilaaf vote kardein, toh Sarpanch ko pad chhodna pad sakta hai.

q: Who is the 'Chief' of the Urban local body in a small town? / छोटे शहर में शहरी स्थानीय निकाय का 'प्रमुख' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Hierarchy: Nagar Panchayat aur Nagar Parishad mein head ko 'Chairman' kehte hain.

q: Under Article 243-J, who provides for the 'Audit' of Panchayats? / अनुच्छेद 243-J के तहत, पंचायतों के 'अंकेक्षण' का प्रावधान कौन करता है?
e: 1. Fact: Har state Assembly apna auditing mechanism khud banati hai.

q: The 'L.M. Singhvi Committee' (1986) suggested? / एल.एम. सिंघवी समिति (1986) ने क्या सुझाव दिया था?
o: Constitutional recognition of Panchayats (पंचायतों को संवैधानिक मान्यता)
e: 1. Importance: Isi committee ne Panchayato ko Samvidhan mein jagah dene ki sabse pehle sifaarish ki thi.

q: Who is the 'Guardian' of the voter list for Municipality elections? / नगर पालिका चुनावों के लिए मतदाता सूची का 'अभिरक्षक' कौन है?
e: 1. Rule: Article 243-ZA ke tehat Municipal elections bhi SEC karwata hai.

q: What is the 'Minimum Population' to have a Municipal Corporation in Bihar? / बिहार में नगर निगम होने के लिए 'न्यूनतम जनसंख्या' क्या है?
e: 1. Bihar Rule: 2 lakh se zyada abadi hone par hi Nagar Nigam banaya jata hai.

q: In Bihar, who is the 'Competent Authority' to hear an appeal against the Mukhiya's decision? / बिहार में मुखिया के निर्णय के विरुद्ध अपील सुनने के लिए 'सक्षम प्राधिकारी' कौन है?
e:
1. Power: Administrative maamlo mein DM hi sabse bada officer hota hai. 
2. Process: Agar Mukhiya kanoon todta hai toh DM uspar jaanch bitha sakta hai.

q: Which Article provides for the 'Audit of Accounts' of Panchayats? / पंचायतों के 'लेखा परीक्षण' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e:
1. Logic: J for Journalism/Journal (Hisaab-kitab). 
2. Fact: State Legislature tay karta hai ki auditor kaun hoga.

q: What is the 'Minimum Population' required for a 'Nagar Panchayat' in Bihar? / बिहार में 'नगर पंचायत' के लिए न्यूनतम जनसंख्या क्या है?
e: 1. Bihar Rule: 12,000 se 40,000 ki abadi transition area (gaon se shehar) ke liye Nagar Panchayat banti hai.

q: Who is the 'Executive Officer' of a Nagar Panchayat in Bihar? / बिहार में नगर पंचायत का 'कार्यपालक पदाधिकारी' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Status: Ye ek Group-B level ka sarkari officer hota hai jo Bihar Administrative Service se aata hai.

q: The 'Gram Katchery' in Bihar has the power to impose a maximum fine of? / बिहार में ग्राम कचहरी अधिकतम कितना जुर्माना लगा सकती है?
e:
1. Post-Mortem: Criminal cases mein Sarpanch maximum ₹1,000 ka fine laga sakta hai. 
2. Note: Civil cases mein ye limit ₹10,000 hai.

q: Who elects the 'Pramukh' of the Panchayat Samiti in Bihar? / बिहार में पंचायत समिति के 'प्रमुख' का चुनाव कौन करता है?
o: Elected Samiti Members (निर्वाचित समिति सदस्यों द्वारा)
e:
1. Process: Janata 'Samiti Member' gunti hai, aur wo sadasya apne beech se ek 'Pramukh' chunte hain. 
2. Logic: Ye ek indirect election hai.

q: Which Constitutional Amendment deals with 'Urban Local Bodies'? / कौन सा संविधान संशोधन 'शहरी स्थानीय निकायों' से संबंधित है?
e: 1. Fact: 1992 mein ye dono amendments sath aaye the. 73rd (Gramin) aur 74th (Shehari).

q: Who is the 'Ex-officio' member of the Zila Parishad in Bihar? / बिहार में जिला परिषद का 'पदेन' सदस्य कौन होता है?
o: All Pramukhs of the District (जिले के सभी प्रमुख)
e: 1. Hierarchy: Block level ke 'Pramukh' automatically Zila Parishad ke member ban jate hain.

q: Under Article 243-D, what is the reservation for 'SC/ST' in Panchayats? / अनुच्छेद 243-D के तहत पंचायतों में 'SC/ST' के लिए आरक्षण कितना है?
o: In proportion to their population (उनकी जनसंख्या के अनुपात में)
e: 1. Rule: Jitni abadi, utna arakshan. Ye mahilaon ke 1/3 (ya 50%) se alag system hai.

q: The 'Nyay Mitra' helps the Sarpanch. What is the minimum 'Qualification' for him? / न्याय मित्र सरपंच की मदद करता है। उसके लिए न्यूनतम 'योग्यता' क्या है?
e: 1. Logic: Kanooni baatein samjhane ke liye kanoon ki degree (LLB) zaroori hai.

q: How many 'Wards' are usually in a Gram Panchayat in Bihar? / बिहार में एक ग्राम पंचायत में आमतौर पर कितने 'वार्ड' होते हैं?
e: 1. Rule: Har 500 ki abadi par ek ward banta hai. Agar abadi 7,000 hai toh lagbhag 14-15 wards honge.

q: Who presides over the 'Gram Sabha' meetings in Bihar? / बिहार में 'ग्राम सभा' की बैठकों की अध्यक्षता कौन करता है?
e: 1. Rule: Mukhiya hi adhyaksha hota hai. Agar wo nahi hai toh Up-Mukhiya.

q: The 'Zila Parishad Adhyaksha' submits his resignation to? / जिला परिषद अध्यक्ष अपना इस्तीफा किसे सौंपता है?
e: 1. Bihar Rule: Zila Parishad ka elected head DM ko apna resignation letter deta hai.

q: Which Article provides for 'District Planning Committees' (DPC)? / 'जिला योजना समितियों' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e: 1. Work: Shehar aur gaon ke vikas plans ko merge karke pure zile ka map banana.

q: In Bihar, 'Sarpanch' is elected for a term of? / बिहार में 'सरपंच' का चुनाव कितनी अवधि के लिए होता है?
e: 1. Rule: Panchayati Raj ke saare pado ka term 5 saal hi hai.

q: Who is the 'Administrative Head' of the Panchayat Samiti? / पंचायत समिति का 'प्रशासनिक प्रमुख' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Post-Mortem: Pramukh chuna hua (elected) hota hai, par BDO sarkari naukar hota hai jo kaam karwata hai.

q: Which Schedule contains '29 items' for Panchayats? / किस अनुसूची में पंचायतों के लिए '29 विषय' शामिल हैं?
e: 1. Fact: 11th Schedule 73rd Amendment se joda gaya tha.

q: Who conducts 'Elections' for Municipalities in Bihar? / बिहार में नगर पालिकाओं के लिए 'चुनाव' कौन आयोजित करता है?
e: 1. Rule: Article 243-ZA ke tehat SEC shehari chunav karwata hai.

q: What is the 'Minimum Age' to be a Mayor in Bihar? / बिहार में मेयर बनने के लिए 'न्यूनतम आयु' क्या है?
e: 1. Logic: Local bodies (Panchayat/Nagar Nigam) ke liye election ladne ki age 21 saal fix hai.

q: The '74th Amendment' added which 'Part' to the Constitution? / 74वें संशोधन ने संविधान में कौन सा 'भाग' जोड़ा?
e: 1. Fact: Part IX Panchayato ke liye aur IX-A Municipalities ke liye.

q: Who provides 'Grants-in-aid' to Panchayats in Bihar? / बिहार में पंचायतों को 'सहायता अनुदान' कौन प्रदान करता है?
e: 1. Process: Finance Commission ki sifaarish par State Govt paisa deti hai.

q: Can 'Gram Sabha' members remove the Mukhiya directly? / क्या ग्राम सभा के सदस्य मुखिया को सीधे हटा सकते हैं?
o: No, through No-Confidence by Ward Members (नहीं, वार्ड सदस्यों के अविश्वास प्रस्ताव द्वारा)
e: 1. Process: Ward members 2/3rd majority se proposal pass karte hain.

q: Which Article defines 'The Municipalities'? / 'नगर पालिकाओं' को कौन सा अनुच्छेद परिभाषित करता है?
e: 1. Fact: P for Parishad/Panchayat definitions (Urban context).

q: Who is the 'Secretary' of the Gram Katchery in Bihar? / बिहार में ग्राम कचहरी का 'सचिव' कौन होता hai?
e: 1. Distinction: Katchery ke liye Nyay Secretary aur Panchayat ke liye Panchayat Secretary.

q: The 'Nagar Nigam' (Municipal Corporation) is for population above? / नगर निगम कितनी जनसंख्या से ऊपर के लिए है? (Bihar Context)
e: 1. Rule: Bihar mein 2 lakh se zyada abadi hone par hi Nigam banta hai.

q: Who is the 'Administrative Head' of the Zila Parishad? / जिला परिषद का 'प्रशासनिक प्रमुख' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Logic: Elected head Adhyaksha, administrative head DDC (IAS).

q: Which Article provides for 'State Finance Commission' (SFC)? / 'राज्य वित्त आयोग' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e: 1. Logic: I for Income/Investment (Finance).

q: Under Article 243-B, 'Panchayats' are at how many levels? / अनुच्छेद 243-B के तहत, पंचायतें कितने स्तरों पर हैं?
e: 1. Levels: Gram, Block (Samiti), Zila (Parishad).

q: Who is the 'Convenor' of the Ward Sabha meetings in Bihar? / बिहार में वार्ड सभा की बैठकों का 'संयोजक' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Logic: Apne ward ki meeting Ward Member hi bulata hai.

q: Which committee suggested 'Constitutional Status' for Panchayats first? / पंचायतों को 'संवैधानिक दर्जा' देने का सुझाव सबसे पहले किस समिति ने दिया?
e: 1. Year: 1986 mein Singhvi committee ne ye rai di thi.

q: Who was the 'First Governor' of Bihar (Azad Bihar era)? / आजाद बिहार काल के पहले राज्यपाल कौन थे?
e: 1. History: 1947 mein unhone pad sambhala tha.

q: What is the 'Quorum' for Gram Sabha meeting in Bihar? / बिहार में ग्राम सभा की बैठक के लिए 'कोरम' क्या है?
e: 1. Bihar Rule: Kam se kam 5% ya 1/20 members hone chahiye tabhi meeting hogi.

q: Which Article bars the 'Interference of Courts' in Panchayat elections? / कौन सा अनुच्छेद पंचायत चुनावों में अदालतों के हस्तक्षेप पर रोक लगाता है?
e: 1. Logic: O for Obstruction/Objection restriction.

q: Who is the 'Political Head' of a small town (Nagar Panchayat)? / छोटे शहर (नगर पंचायत) का 'राजनीतिक प्रमुख' कौन होता है?
e: 1. Hierarchy: Mayor sirf Nagar Nigam (bade shehar) mein hota hai.

q: Under Article 243-D, what is the reservation for 'Women' in Bihar? / अनुच्छेद 243-D के तहत, बिहार में महिलाओं के लिए आरक्षण कितना है?
e: 1. Bihar Stat: Central law 33% kehta hai, par Bihar pehla state tha jisne 50% diya.

q: Who appoints the 'Sarpanch' in Bihar? / बिहार में सरपंच की नियुक्ति कौन करता है?
e: 1. Fact: Sarpanch ko janata vote dekar chunti hai (Elected, not Appointed).

q: Which Article provides for the 'Constitution of Ward Committees'? / 'वार्ड समितियों' के गठन का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e: 1. Rule: 3 lakh se zyada abadi wale sheharo mein ye mandatory hai.

q: The 'Nyay Mitra' provides legal help. Is he a lawyer? / न्याय मित्र कानूनी मदद देता है। क्या वह एक वकील है?
e: 1. Fact: Nyay Mitra ko kanoon ki jankari honi zaroori hai.
